data.title: Enbridge: Valued Like An AI Tech Company (Downgrade)
data.description:
Enbridge's (ENB) Q4 earnings release was solid. The company continues demonstrating stable growth in distributable cash flow even though the CapEx program is ramping up aggressively. I was lucky to lock in a notably more attractive dividend yield than the current 5.3% level, as the stock has rallied by 13% since my previous bullish call. Enbridge's fundamental strength makes me confident in holding this winner further. However, it is currently priced to perfection, and I am not ready to buy it while it is that expensive.

On the other hand, there is a good idea for income investors who missed out on the moment when ENB's dividend yield was much higher. The company has various issues of preference shares, and the one that looks particularly interesting is an instrument called 'Cumulative Redeemable Preference Shares Series 1' (EBBGF), which currently offers a much higher 6.7% forward yield compared to common stock. To sum up, I downgrade common stock ENB to a 'Hold' because it is currently priced for perfection while I see increasing risks due to the rapidly expanding CapEx program.

Q4 Earnings Review

The company released its Q4 earnings last Friday, on February 13th. It was a solid quarter as ENB delivered another double earnings beat, highlighting that the Q3 EPS miss was just a lone hiccup and not a trend. The Q4 beat was strong, with a massive $3.3 billion revenue surprise and a $0.08 non-GAAP EPS surprise. Enbridge's adjusted EBITDA increased both on a Q4 (1.6% up) and full-year (7% up) basis, which inevitably positively affected the distributable cash flow ('DCF').

The company's full-year DCF per share increased by 2.6%, which is decent growth given Enbridge's aggressive investments in growth projects in 2025. According to the company's cash flow statement, its net CapEx increased from $4.7 billion in 2024 to $6.5 billion in 2025, a massive 38% increase. Therefore, I believe that the management's capital allocation remains strong as the company continues to invest in growth projects aggressively while maintaining the DCF per share growth. With that being said, I believe that ENB's 5.3% forward dividend yield is safe.

The current yield might be quite attractive for some investors, as it is notably higher than the risk-free rate. However, I have been buying this stock since its dividend yield was above 7%, so for me the 5.3% yield is not that attractive. By the way, for investors who agree with me that ordinary shares' yield is not very attractive, I recommend paying attention to EBBGF, which currently offers a hefty 6.7% yield. These are preferred shares, which means they enjoy superior dividend coverage compared to common shares, as their payouts are prioritized in the company's capital structure. EBBGF currently trades at $24.85 per share and offers a quarterly dividend of $0.41898.

Besides robust Q4 and full-year performance, the management also released solid guidance, which is another bullish sign. The management expects growth in adjusted EBITDA and DCF per share in 2026, which will continue supporting dividend payouts to investors.

On the other hand, there is one warning sign. The company continues aggressively expanding its CapEx backlog as it ended FY2025 with $39 billion in total secured capital program. As of my previous ENB article, which I shared after the Q3 earnings, the capital program amounted to $35 billion. It implies that within just one quarter, Q4, Enbridge's capital program amount increased by more than 10%. Meanwhile, even if the company achieves the higher end of its adjusted FY2026 EBITDA guidance of $20.8 billion, it will be a 4% growth compared to FY2025. The fact that the CapEx budget is growing much more aggressively than the FY2026 adjusted EBITDA projected growth means elevated profitability compression risks for investors over the long term. Of course, Enbridge has a stellar proven record of success, and the industry itself enjoys AI infrastructure tailwinds, but aggressively ramping CapEx is always a big risk due to long payback periods for midstream infrastructure projects.

Valuation Analysis

Enbridge's current P/E ratio of above 20 is high for a midstream company because the industry itself is mature and it is not growing fast. Even if the company achieves the projected EPS growth as outlined in the table below, the estimated FY2029 P/E of almost 19 is still quite high for a midstream company. To give readers more understanding, just picture the fact that NVIDIA's (NVDA) estimated forward P/E ratio for FY2029 is 16.5 at the moment.

Please do not get me wrong here. I know that comparing Enbridge to Nvidia is like comparing apples to oranges. What I want to spotlight here is the fact that sometimes we hear and read opinions that 'AI is a bubble' and 'the semiconductor industry is a bubble.' And here we see that Enbridge's forward P/E ratio trajectory is comparable to the largest representative of the semiconductor industry.

If you don't like the Nvidia comparison, let's be more traditional in selecting benchmarks and compare ENB's valuation against its direct rivals, other large North American energy midstream companies. As the above comparison suggests, ENB is significantly more expensive across almost all metrics. ENB's premium valuation could have been justified if it had unparalleled growth, profitability, or recent EPS revisions. However, the below comparison suggests that ENB's Quant factor grades across these dimensions are comparable to peers. Therefore, its premium valuation is not justified.

Meanwhile, the current $24.85 share price of EBBGF looks attractive as the price is currently slightly cheaper than the $25 par value. Moreover, its dividend yield of 6.7% is twice as high as the sector median of 3.35%, which also underscores the attractiveness of the valuation.

Risks of Investing in EBBGF

While EBBGF's valuation looks good at the moment, it is important to understand that it doesn't mean there is significant upside potential. The fact that it is a redeemable instrument with a $25 par value means that the company can redeem these shares at par at any time. Therefore, the stock is extremely unlikely to ever climb above its par value. It means that EBBGF doesn't offer investors share price appreciation at the moment. With that being said, EBBGF is not a very good investment for people seeking capital gains.

It is also vital to understand that EBBGF is traded OTC, meaning much lower liquidity. The average daily trading volume is around 3,000 shares, while ENB's average daily volume is somewhere around 20 million shares. Therefore, EBBGF investors might face high bid-ask spreads, which might offset some portion of the aforementioned 6.7% dividend yield. Moreover, it might lead to delayed execution of your selling orders as fewer investors actively trade this security.

Conclusion

I believe that ENB is currently priced to perfection, which means that buying it despite robust Q4 earnings and positive FY2026 guidance is not a great deal. I think it is a 'Hold' at the moment. Meanwhile, I suggest investors pay attention to the EBBGF instrument, which is currently traded slightly below par value and offers a compelling 6.7% forward dividend yield. I am not buying EBBGF because I seek capital appreciation as well, and I believe that I am too young (under 40) to pursue high yield only. However, I think that Enbridge's stellar fundamentals support the safety of EBBGF's yield, and this stock is likely to be a good investment opportunity for income investors who are closer to retirement and seek lower risk and lower volatility with superior yield.

data.title: Dividend Harvesting Portfolio Week 259: $25,900 Allocated, $2,793.02 In Projected Dividends
data.description:
It wasn’t the greatest week in the market, and the narrative regarding AI continued to impact technology companies, especially those focused on SaaS. The S&P 500 fell -1.4%, while the Nasdaq declined by -2.13% this week. It feels like the new momentum trade is rotation, as investors seem to be shifting their focus to companies that have less of a chance of being disrupted by AI. Capital seems to be flowing to companies that are going to utilize AI to expand margins rather than the companies embedding it into their products. Regardless of how anyone feels about software companies, the narrative can’t be proven or disproven, and the State Street SPDR S&P Software & Services ETF (XSW) is down -20.60%, while individual names like Salesforce (CRM) have declined -25.29% over the past month. I have no idea how long this will last, and even Microsoft (MSFT) is down 15.15%, as not even the megacaps are safe. We’re heading into the end of earnings season, and everyone is looking at NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) after hearing what the megacaps plan on allocating toward CapEx, and if Jensen Huang can deliver, I believe it could help the market regain confidence in the technology trade. I think that the selloff is overdone, and we will look back on this period as a great buying opportunity.

After allocating $25,900 to the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio, the account balance finished week 259 at $36. The account's profitability increased (7.97%) to $9,978.19, which is a return on invested capital of 38.68%. This week, I added to my positions in the Amplify CWP Growth & Income ETF (QDVO) and NNN REIT (NNN). The Dividend Harvesting Portfolio generated $40 of dividend income in week 259. The combination of reinvesting the generated dividend income and making these investments increased the forward projected annualized dividend income by $8.72 (0.31%) to $2,793.02. The Dividend Harvesting Portfolio has generated $5,957.74 of income since its inception, which is 23% of the total capital I have allocated toward the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio. I can’t believe we’re already halfway through February and earnings season is almost over. Corporate earnings have been strong, and the recent CPI report showed inflation cooling in January to 2.4%. 2026 is off to a bumpy start, but there is a lot of time left in 2026, and I believe we are going to finish the year significantly higher than we are today.

The Overall Performance of the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio

Over the years, there have been many comments criticizing this portfolio, from the investment style to the number of positions. This is not a technology-heavy portfolio, as it's focused on recurring income and diversification. Currently, many names in the technology sector are having a difficult time, and in a week where the Nasdaq is down -2.13%, the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio’s profitability was up 2.23% as another new high was established. My main goals have been to create a portfolio that can mitigate downside risk and generate recurring income. There has been $25,900 allocated toward the portfolio, and it finished the week with a balance of $36,100.69, which is an increase of $10,200.69 (39.38%). The portfolio is now generating $2,793.02 in forward dividend income, and the year is just getting started. This portfolio has navigated several difficult environments and continues to prove that it is built to accomplish my goals. I am looking forward to continuing to allocate capital toward it and building out the ongoing stream of income.

The Dividend Harvesting Portfolio Dividend Section

Here's how much dividend income is generated per investment basket:

  • ETFs $930.26 (33.31%)
  • Equities $638.77 (22.87%)
  • CEFs $470.49 (16.85%)
  • REITs $476.47 (17.06%)
  • BDCs $277.02 (9.92%)

Investors utilize dividends to supplement their income and live off them. I'm building a dividend portfolio for myself for 30 years in the future. In 2022, I collected $507.80 in dividend income from 533 dividends. In 2023, I collected $978.11 in dividend income from 660 dividends. 2024 was a great year, as the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio generated $1,623.14 in dividend income from 709 dividends. In 2025, I collected $2,411.27 in dividend income from 831 dividends. After 6 weeks in 2026, $411.03 of dividend income was produced, which is 17.05% of the total income from 2025. There have been 98 dividends generated in 2026, which is 11.79% of the dividends generated in 2025.

There was $40 of dividend income generated this week, which brought the average amount of dividend income I have generated on a weekly basis to $58.72. This is an increase of $12.35 (26.63%) YoY. When I look at the beginning of 2026 compared to 2025, I believe that the income path is going to intensify by a large amount. Over the next several years, I should be able to exceed $5,000 in forward dividend income in this account and basically double my annualized allocation to the portfolio.

The 2nd week in February is finished, and I have generated $101.29 of income, which is 70.48% of the dividends that were generated last year. I had projected that there was a chance I could reach $200 this month even though it is a short month with a holiday, and I still think that can happen. I’m getting very excited about this portfolio because when I look at the data, February generated the least amount of income in 2025. If I can get close to $200 or exceed it, I believe that the portfolio will be in a position to exceed $250 in monthly income sometime in 2026.

Realty Income (O) has been running and is no longer generating 1 share per year from its dividend income. There are now 39 positions in the table below that are accomplishing this. There's $328.55 of forward dividend income being generated from the new dividends being produced from all the positions generating at least one share from their dividends annually. Many positions are still lagging, but I'm making a strong effort to allocate capital toward them so I can get at least 50% of the portfolio to accomplish this goal by the end of the year. My fixation on this is because it will allow me to advance the compounding impacts from the dividend snowball, and it also makes sure that I am not investing in the same positions each week.

The Dividend Harvesting Portfolio Composition

ETFs are exceeding 20% of the portfolio again, and it's entirely my fault. I was also adding REITs heading into the rate cut, which caused the portfolio to get a bit top-heavy. It's difficult for me to stay away from these sectors, especially after big tech crushed earnings. I'm going to work on evening out the sectors and having them more proportional in the future. Individual equities now represent 41.30% of the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio while generating 22.87% of the dividend income. REITs, ETFs, CEFs, and BDCs make up 58.70% of the portfolio and generate 77.13% of the forward income. I'm working on getting individual equities to represent more of the portfolio. The long-term goal is to have sectors close to equal weight, but that may not happen for 5-10 years at this rate, if ever. I have been adding energy companies as I believe there is a big opportunity there, and I will probably continue allocating capital toward energy positions as time progresses.

Altria Group (MO) is starting to run again, and no matter how long I have added capital for without buying more MO, it’s still above 4% of the portfolio. This has been an incredible investment, as it's now been 2 years since I bought shares. My allocation to the top 20 positions looks good in my eyes, as it has been leveling off, and I still believe there is a lot of value to be unlocked. Over the next several months I hope I can have this chart look a bit more even, but I will add to these positions when opportunities present themselves.

This week the top-10 holdings changed again as Citigroup (C) was knocked off the list and AT&T (T) made it back on. This is my favorite table I create because it shows every detail of these investments. For instance, MO has already created 31.30% of the capital I invested from its dividends. My allocation to the top 10 positions is $7,149.74, and they finished the week with a value of $10,378.80. This is an ROI of $3,229.06 (45.16%). There has been $1,596.91 of dividends generated and reinvested, which is 22.34% of the initial investment into these positions. I'm now projecting that $758.80 of dividend income will be generated from the top 10 holdings, which have a forward yield of 10.61%. These positions represent 28.75% of the portfolio, while their forward dividend income is projected to generate 27.17% of the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio's annualized income.

Week 259 Additions

This week I added to my positions in the Amplify CWP Growth & Income ETF and NNN REIT.

Amplify CWP Growth & Income ETF

  • QDVO delivers a compelling blend of double-digit yield and capital appreciation and is outperforming similar ETFs in the sector
  • I am a fan of the tactical covered call strategy as it targets 4–6% option premium plus 0–2% dividend yield, which fits well in the portfolio
  • QDVO’s concentrated portfolio, heavily weighted toward undervalued technology leaders, positions QDVO for continued outperformance as risk-free rates decline

NNN REIT

  • NNN has been breaking out recently, and I think that it is still undervalued as it is trading at less than 13 times its FFO
  • NNN still has a dividend yield of 5.41% that is supported by 35 years of annualized dividend increases
  • NNN is one of my favorite REITs, as its exposure is to single-tenant properties with triple net leases, so all expenses are on the tenant
  • NNN has 10-20 year initial lease terms, with only 8.4% of their leases expiring through 2027, which creates durable revenue

Week 260 Gameplan

It’s that time again, and I need more suggestions for the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio. Please leave all of your suggestions in the comment section because I will be adding a position from all of the ideas the community has provided.

Conclusion

February is turning out to be a strong month for dividend income, as I have crossed over the $100 level after the first 2 weeks. The Dividend Harvesting Portfolio has made new all-time highs and crossed over the $36,000 level for the first time. Part of the technology segment is declining, and many of the high-beta names that retail has allocated capital toward are on a downward descent. The Dividend Harvesting Portfolio was built to operate in any investment cycle, and it’s living up to my expectations. With $2,793.02 of dividend income being generated, the account is able to plow more than half of my annualized allocations back into the portfolio. The current market activity is showcasing that sometimes boring investing works, and the Dividend Harvesting Portfolio is at all-time highs while many tech investors are experiencing drawdowns. Please leave all of your comments below, as I enjoy interacting with everyone in the comment section.

data.title: John Hancock 2040 Lifetime Blend Portfolio Q4 2025 Commentary
data.description:
Quarterly commentary

  • Most asset categories produced solid returns in the final three months of 2025, reflecting the favorable backdrop for the world financial markets.
  • The fund posted a gain but slightly underperformed its benchmark.
  • Asset allocation contributed to performance, while underlying manager results detracted.

Market review and outlook

Global equities registered solid gains in the fourth quarter, helping the major, broad-based indexes record their third consecutive year of double-digit returns. Performance was uneven over the first half of the quarter due to concerns that AI-related stocks were in a bubble, but the market staged an impressive rebound and went on to achieve new all-time highs by year end. A continued decline in inflation enabled the U.S. Federal Reserve to enact two quarter-point interest rate cuts, boosting sentiment. In addition, corporate earnings were robust and world economic growth remained positive. Emerging- and developed-market international equities outperformed the United States, continuing a trend that was in place for the full year. Within the U.S. market, the value style outpaced growth as investors rotated toward opportunities outside of AI-related stocks.

Global bonds logged only slightly positive total returns amid a growing consensus that most central banks were largely finished easing policy. Credit-oriented market segments continued to outperform, primarily as a result of their yield advantage.

Contributors and detractors

The fund's overweight in equities versus bonds contributed to relative performance. An overweight in emerging-market stocks, the best-performing segment of the global equity markets in the quarter, was a further plus. On the other hand, the allocation to defensive equities was a modest detractor at a time in which more speculative stocks were in favor. An overweight in U.S. mid caps also cost the fund some relative performance. Positioning in fixed income had a neutral effect on results. An allocation to emerging-market bonds performed well, as the asset class strongly outperformed its developed-market peers in an environment of falling interest rates and rising commodity prices. Conversely, a position in U.S. Treasury STRIPS detracted amid a broader increase in long-term bond yields.

No single area stood out as a key driver of underperformance for the fund's active managers; instead, the shortfall was primarily a result of very small differences for a wide range of individual holdings.

Portfolio changes

We retained diversified exposure across the full spectrum of the world financial markets, while also making some modest changes on the margin to capitalize on categories where we saw attractive valuations and higher long-term return potential. We remain confident in the fund's positioning as we move into 2026, based on our belief that diversification is likely to become increasingly important. For most of the past year, investors would have been rewarded by simply holding the narrow group of mega-cap U.S. technology-related stocks that continued to dominate performance. However, we began to see this backdrop evolve somewhat over the final three months of the year with a notable broadening of leadership. While it's impossible to say if this is a sign of a larger shift, we believe investors are better served by maintaining broad-based diversification rather than relying on another year of positive relative performance for a handful of companies. We think the fund is well positioned for these circumstances through its ability to seek opportunities across a wide range of asset classes.

data.title: Zoetis: Why I'm Doubling Down On My Worst 2025 Pick
data.description:
One year ago, I rated Zoetis (ZTS) a 'buy' following its Q4 2024 earnings report. After the stock fell in response to lower-than-expected guidance, I argued that management was being overly conservative and the dip presented a buying opportunity. Unfortunately, Zoetis turned out to be my worst stock pick when I reviewed my 2025 year-end performance. The stock fell -23% in the past year, badly lagging the S&P 500 and single-handedly reducing my average return by nearly 200 basis points to 34.3%. Biotech had an exceptional year in 2025, so it stung that my worst performing pick wasn't a high-risk/high-reward speculative play. I tried to play it safe, but got burned anyway.

Thus, a mea culpa is in order. I viewed Zoetis as a steady compounder, but it became a fallen growth stock. I thought that after the stock had peaked, reaching an all-time closing high of $247/share in late-2021, after over three years of consolidation, the company was primed to take another run at all-time highs if growth beat management's 'conservative' outlook.

Frustratingly, Zoetis did outperform its 2025 outlook, notching $9.5 billion in 2025 sales versus the guidance range of $9.23-$9.38 billion that crashed the stock in early 2024. Due to the forward-looking logic of the market, that outperformance doesn't matter. It is in the past, and markets only care about the future.

Now after Zoetis' Q4 2025 earnings report, it feels like Groundhog Day, with me playing the part of a disgruntled Bill Murray. Guidance disappointed yet again, with the 2026 outlook targeting a range that may exceed $10 billion. However, shares that opened sharply lower quickly rebounded. I am doubling down on my worst pick from last year because Zoetis keeps growing, margins and ROIC are excellent, and now the valuation is more compelling. Lastly, the stock shrugging off the disappointing guidance hints that sellers may have become exhausted. Moving forward, I believe that Zoetis can exceed its conservative outlook; therefore, I reiterate my buy rating for 2026.

Zoetis: Q4 2024 Earnings Presentation and 2025 Guidance

In the fourth quarter, revenue reached $2.39B, growing 3.0% YoY and slightly beating the analyst consensus by $30M. Adjusted EPS of $1.48/share beat expectations by $0.08/share, an increase of 5.7% from Q4 2024. For the fiscal year, revenue grew by 2.6% YoY to $9.5B, while adjusted earnings grew 8.3%. A bright spot in the report was expanding gross margins, which reached 83.5% for FY 2025 from 81.6% in 2024. Expanding margins provided reasonable EPS growth even as sales growth was challenged.

The market's initial disappointment came in regard to the outlook for 2026. 2026 guidance was for a midpoint of $9.925B, in line with the consensus estimate of $9.91B. This leads to the conclusion that Zoetis is better positioned than one year ago. In 2025, guidance missed consensus estimates by 2.8% at the midpoint. Now it is comparable; therefore, if Zoetis again under-promises and over-delivers, the stock is set to surprise to the upside.

Gross Margin 67.4% 67.7% -- -- 83.5% 81.6%
Zoetis Q4 and FY Highlights* Q4 2025 Q4 2024 FY 2026 (consensus est.) FY 2026 (guidance) FY 2025 FY 2024
Revenue $2.39B $2.32B $9.91B $9.825 - $10.025B $9.5B $9.26B
Non-GAAP EPS $1.48 $1.40 $7.01 $7.00 - $7.10 $6.41 $5.92
GAAP EPS $1.37 $1.29 -- $6.65 - $6.75 $6.02 $5.47

*From Seeking Alpha and Zoetis' Q4 2025 Press Release.

On the earnings call, CEO Kristin Peck began by emphasizing the company's good performance in 2025. While revenue growth slowed, this was offset by expanding margins, which drove high single-digit growth on the bottom line. Moving forward, weakness in the U.S. dollar is now a tailwind, with foreign exchange providing a favorable impact of 80 basis points. $4.1B was returned to shareholders via dividends and buybacks, with the share count shrinking to 422.1M in Q4 2025, compared to 447.8M (a 5.74% YoY reduction). Zoetis is very shareholder-friendly and a good steward of capital.

Geographically, $5.1B or 53.7%, of revenues came from the US. This represented 4% YoY growth. The remainder of international revenues grew to $4.3B, an 8% YoY growth rate.

In the US, revenue from companion animals was 4.67x that of livestock, while internationally these segments were closer to parity (1.26x). This shows the opportunity in the coming years for growth, as consumers entering the middle class should have more room to spend on pets.

The company's global franchise, Simparica, grew at 12% operationally on the ongoing shift to triple combination treatments, but this was considerably slower than the 28% YoY growth in 2024. While sales topped $1.5B, this growth driver has slowed considerably and is not as significant as previously.

The surprise winner relative to expectations in 2025 was international livestock. While the entire livestock segment grew by 8% YoY, this was driven by 10% growth in international markets. This highlights the growing demand for livestock in emerging markets as consumers enter the middle class and consume more protein. Thus, Zoetis has a secular tailwind in this sector that may persist for many years.

Zoetis has the industry's most robust pipeline, with management identifying up to 12 blockbusters in development (defined as >$100M annual sales). These new product catalysts will arrive steadily over the coming years. Portela and Lenivia have received or are nearing approval for the treatment of osteoporosis in dogs and cats. This year, two additional blockbusters may be approved, with 8 more coming in 2027 and beyond.

The earnings call highlighted the large total addressable market [TAM], which could potentially fuel up to $9.1B in sales growth over the coming years. These drugs in kidney disease, oncology, cardiology, obesity, and anxiety could fuel substantial sales growth as consumers in developed and emerging markets opt to spend more in order to take better care of their pets.

Overall, the tone of management on the call seemed cautiously confident, while analysts were neutral to slightly negative, expressing concerns about competitive headwinds and pricing. While there are certainly business headwinds in the year ahead, the market is more conservatively discounting them compared with last year.

Zoetis: Risk Factors Highlighted for 2026

The earnings call also highlighted risks to Zoetis' business in the coming year.

Some of these risk factors include:

  1. Gen Z/Millennials have reduced vet visits, with management attributing this to tighter household budgets.
  2. Lenivia FDA approval was delayed in the US. While the drug is launching in 1H 2026 in Canada and the EU, the US is delayed until 2027.
  3. Osteoarthritis [OA] declined 3% YoY, in part due to delays in FDA approval. Thus, growth in OA has been challenged recently.
  4. Zoetis' Apoquel (oclacitinib), an oral JAK inhibitor, faces competition from the launch of "two oral JAK competitors."
  5. As nearly half of sales are international, a strengthening US dollar is a risk factor. However, the dollar has been weaker recently.

While there are risks, Zoetis' stock seems to be discounting them rather harshly. This may present a wall of worry for Zoetis to climb in 2026.

Zoetis: Discounted Cash Flow and Consensus Expectations

The Wall Street consensus now expects 4.7% revenue growth in the coming year and approximately 3-5% growth through 2032. This targets approximately $13.2B in revenue by then, a conservative figure given the company's pipeline. At the present time, investors are getting the pipeline for free, and only slow growth is expected in line with the low end of recent performance. Given the headwinds above, I believe this is a relatively pessimistic view, and sooner or later the stock should outperform.

Zoetis has fantastic margins and operational leverage that have allowed the company to grow earnings at a faster rate than sales. Moving forward, this is expected to continue. While the dividend yield is currently 1.60%, dividend growth has averaged 19.0% over the past 5 years. The consensus sees double-digit dividend growth in the coming years, driven by increasing EPS and a low payout ratio of approximately 31%. Zoetis is therefore a fantastic dividend growth company.

Compared to last year, more conservative estimates place the price of Zoetis at a 20% discount to fair value. Projecting cash from operations and CapEx to grow at 7% annually (in line with EPS), a 10% cost of capital, and 2.5% growth in perpetuity, Zoetis presents a value of $158.81/share. My 12-month price target is $159/share, in line with the company's fair value.

Over the past year, Zoetis's earnings multiple has compressed greatly, from 28.2x a year ago, to 18.1x today. In terms of price/sales and EV/EBITDA, the stock is as cheap as it has been in the past 10 years. Even during the panic lows of COVID in 2020. While the price is still higher, earnings and sales have grown considerably since then.

There are many assumptions in the above model. However, they seem to be in line with the company's historical performance, indicating that Zoetis is undervalued at the present time. The company's dependable and high-quality earnings, along with substantial growth, are very attractive for a long-term dividend growth investor. Furthermore, continued growth is a bet on a growing middle class in developing markets and continued demand for livestock in the U.S. and abroad.

Conclusion

The primary driver for my continuing Buy rating on Zoetis is simple: the price is cheaper and the expectations are lower. Last year, the market was paying 28x earnings for a growth story that had hit a speed bump. Today, paying 18x earnings, a P/E not seen since the pandemic low, suggests that the market has priced in a worst-case scenario based on threats to the business that are manageable.

The business is resilient; while US consumers have retrenched on spending, Zoetis has continued to grow based on strength in international livestock. Capital discipline, a declining share-count and margin expansion add to this bullish view. While we wait for the US to transition back to growth, downside risk is greatly cushioned by the current valuation and share repurchases.

Based on my updated DCF analysis, fair value lies around $159/share, 20% upside from current levels. I am willing to risk a repeat of 2025 given the potential that sooner or later this quality compounder will break out. Thus, I reiterate Zoetis as a buy.

data.title: NovaGold Moves Towards Bankable Feasibility Study For Donlin Amidst Soaring Gold
data.description:
NovaGold Resources Stays on the Uptrend

NovaGold Resources Inc. (NG) seems well positioned to reap the benefits of rising gold prices during this seemingly endless period of gains, as the yellow metal, barring some short-term jitters, always has ample underlying strength providing support. And to our confirmation of a buy signal upgrading from our previous article.

As Trading Economics writes at the time of writing, these underlying sources of support for the yellow metal consist of:

[…], concerns about currency debasement, ongoing central bank purchases, and geopolitical tensions continue to provide underlying support for the metal. […].

Driven by the need to reckon with and confront a global environment that will never be ideal for economic stability and geopolitical peace, the current situation after about three years of heading towards a gold rush is that these underlying forces are now poised to further catalyze the price of precious metals. Vancouver, Canada-based NovaGold Resources Inc. fits into this scenario. NovaGold is focused on the exploration and development of its flagship project: a 60% interest in the Donlin Gold Project. This project comprises 493 mineral claims covering approximately 29,008 hectares (according to the "Company Profile" of Seeking Alpha) and is located 20 km north of Crooked Creek in the Kuskokwim and Mt. McKinley, US mining districts of Alaska.

Positive Correlation

The positive correlation between the gold price (measured by the Gold Spot Price) and the shares of NovaGold Resources Inc. on the NYSE makes it easier to predict whether this stock will play a leading role in the US gold exploration and development stock market. A positive correlation means that NG's shares are highly likely to rise (fall) when the gold price rises (falls). As a reminder, a positive correlation describes the direction of two trends-whether they tend to move in the same direction (positive) or diverge (negative).

Don't confuse this indicator with returns: This indicator says nothing about the cumulative returns of the two securities over time, which can differ significantly. The correlation is clearly positive: the yellow area (the lower quadrant of the diagram) has been almost always above the 0 line (zero) over the last 12 months.

We Update Our View On NovaGold

To continue benefiting from the positive performance of NovaGold Resources' share price, we are updating our analysis to determine the extent to which correlation indicators are amplified by factors fostering market dynamics that could potentially favor a higher NovaGold share price in rising gold prices; this currently appears to be the case, supporting the buy signal for this stock.

Market participants seeking a deeper understanding of NovaGold's growth prospects will be particularly interested in the success of the company's efforts to complete a bankable feasibility study for future gold production. In our view, progress in this area, as well as ongoing risk mitigation measures, should have a positive impact on the share price.

From Our Previous Coverage

We were positive about NovaGold Resources Inc. as the company outperformed the broader US stock market, due to an increase in its stake in the Donlin gold project (from 50% to 60%).

Highlights from the Donlin Gold mine: 1.13 million ounces of gold annually for 27 years with operating costs of $635/ounce and high-grade gold (2.24 g/t, more than double the global average). Strong exploration potential, as described in our previous analysis.

Based on the technical report prepared in accordance with NI 43-101 dated November 30, 2025, and the technical report summary prepared in accordance with S-K 1300 dated November 30, 2025:

  • NovaGold has measured and indicated mineral resources of 23.99 million ounces, representing a 60% share. This includes reserves.
  • NovaGold has proven and probable reserves of approximately 19.68 million ounces at a grade of 2.02 g/t, representing a 60% share.

NovaGold's 60% stake in Donlin Gold had a Net Present Value of $10.32 billion (5% discount rate) at the time, compared to a market value of $1.58 billion for the NG shares, which may very likely have boosted sentiment.

Based on the acquisition of Barrick Mining's 50% interest in the Donlin Gold project by NovaGold Resources and Paulson Advisers for US$1 billion (a 10% addition to NovaGold's existing 50% interest and a 40% addition to Paulson's existing US$800 million interest of 40%): We valued NovaGold's 60% interest in Donlin at US$1.2 billion, or US$2.95 per share, compared to NG's market price of US$3.70 per share at that time. This discrepancy was seen as possibly contributing to some negative sentiment but potentially providing an opportunity for investors to consolidate their positions. But NovaGold's shares rose 145% in market value, outperforming the S&P 500 by 11.26% since our previous article.

The Donlin Gold project also secured US$310 million in public equity financing for its development, which is just one of the steps towards its continued progress.

Construction and Commercial Production

Construction is scheduled to begin in 2027, with commercial production expected to commence in 2031. According to a presentation by NovaGold from January 2026, the initial investment costs are estimated at approximately US$7.4 billion (100% basis) and are spread across the development and construction phases of the project. We are currently in the development and feasibility study phase for the Donlin Gold project.

Strengthening Market Confidence in Act

We report on the progress of the Donlin gold project's feasibility study, but first, the key results from the drilling come to the fore.

Donlin Gold's 2025 drilling program has delivered results that strengthen the company's position. NovaGold Resources stated in a press release ("PR") dated November 25, 2025, that the Donlin Gold project is:

one of the largest and highest-quality gold projects globally.

In a comment on Nov. 25, 2025, PR, Greg Lang, President and CEO of NovaGold, predicted the beginning of the era of bankable feasibility studies (BFS):

To date, the drilling at Donlin has yielded terrific results, […] The 2025 drill program continues to exceed expectations and provide key data points for the Bankable Feasibility Study (BFS), which is expected to commence by the first quarter of 2026.

On this front, Fluor Corporation will complete the bankable feasibility study by 2027. Fluor is overseeing the key infrastructure components, including the power plant and pipeline, as well as site preparation.

This represents a significant step toward project development, including,

A) The letter of intent from Donlin Gold - Glenfarne Alaska LNG, LLC to supply up to 50 million cubic feet of gas per day to the Donlin gold mine via an approvable new pipeline to power Donlin.

B) The appointment of multi-year expert Frank Arcese as project director at Donlin Gold LLC. On the occasion of Frank Arcese's appointment, NovaGold's President and CEO stated:

His exceptional track record in delivering major mining projects safely, on time, and on budget, combined with his proven expertise in complex engineering projects and environments, will be invaluable as we strengthen Donlin Gold's execution capabilities. With the winds now at our backs, his hiring marks an important internal milestone for Donlin Gold as we continue to build on the team to help ensure full preparedness for the next steps ahead.

Triggered by Favorable Court Rulings

Legal successes pave the way for the development of Donlin Gold. The company stated in the Nov. 25, 2025 PR that:

Alaska Supreme Court Upholds Key State Permits: The Court affirmed Donlin Gold's water rights for the mine and State Right-Of-Way ("State ROW") lease for its proposed natural gas pipeline, validating the State of Alaska's rigorous review and enabling the project to move forward responsibly.

De-Risk factor

The company stated in Nov. 25, 2025 PR that:

Federal Permits in Hand with Acceptance into the Fixing America's Surface Transportation Act (FAST-41).

[…]

This designation is expected to result in a more efficient permitting timeline and reflects the strategic importance of Donlin Gold as a responsible and key economic development project in Alaska.

Approval of FAST-41 could remove the remaining hesitations and lift the lid on funding. The company stated in the Nov. 25, 2025 PR that:

This transparent, science-based review is designed to provide both the public and decision-makers with complete and accurate information. While most projects that are accepted into FAST-41 program are at the initial stages of permitting, Donlin Gold has been deeply engaged in permitting for over a decade and has received most of its state and federal permits.

Money Flowing In Donlin

The above developments are essential for the market to gain greater confidence in the project's viability. In this respect, we see another significant contribution in favor of NovaGold. The company raised capital on February 5, 2026, through an upsized capital offering, privately placing 31,020,000 common shares at a price of US$10.00 per share, for gross proceeds of approximately US$310 million. These proceeds will be used to operate Donlin Gold and to exercise the early repayment option under the loan agreement with Barrick Mining Corporation. Ultimately, as NovaGold Resources gains traction on the stock market, this paves the way for securing more external financing for Donlin Gold, which is crucial in its transformation phase: from a hidden gem amidst Alaska's rich natural resources with enormous potential to a fully-fledged open-pit mine extracting large quantities of gold-bearing rock.

NG NovaGold Resources on the NYSE

NovaGold Resources Inc.'s NYSE-listed shares under the symbol "NG" were trading at $10.72/share, giving it a market capitalization of $4.45 billion, as of the writing of the article.

Technical Analysis

The current share price is 49.4% above the 52-week middle point of $7.175 per share. The 52-week range is from $2.26 to $12.09 per share. The 20-day moving average is above the 50-day moving average, which in turn is above the 100-day moving average, which is above the 200-day moving average. This indicates a positive market trend in the short, medium, and long term. The RSI of 54.51 signals further upside potential before overbought conditions are reached.

Valuation

Gold Spot Price (XAUUSD:CUR) is $5,027.08 (+16.37% year-to-date and +71.60% over the last 12 months), as of this writing.

Seeking Alpha reported the latest gold price forecasts from leading ANZ analysts on Friday, February 13, 2026, and Seeking Alpha wrote:

[…] they reiterate their view that gold remains an insurance asset against a plethora of uncertainties, and the recent correction presents an opportunity for fresh investment. Have revised up price forecasts, with gold now expected to hit $5,800/oz in Q2 2026 (previously $5,400/oz).

In a Cartesian coordinate system, we plot the gold price per ounce (the independent variable) on the x-axis (X). We plot the Net Present Value (at 5% discount rate), the dependent variable on X, on the y-axis (Y). Then, we draw the regression line (linear equation) that optimally connects the different pairs of values ​​ (X;Y) in the plane. Next, we calculate the coefficients (a; b) of the equation Y = a*X + b. This gives us the algebraic equation of the line: Y = 0.0077X - 10.919.

Finally, we substitute the gold prices of $5,027.08/oz (current gold price) and $5,800/oz (price forecast by ANZ analysts) for X and provide the corresponding Y values.

With a gold price of $5,027.08/oz, the calculated net present value (NPV, 5%) is approximately $27.8 billion.

At a gold price of $5,800/ounce, the calculated NPV, 5% is approximately $33.7 billion.

Since NovaGold Resources holds 60% of the shares in Donlin Gold, the attributable NPV, 5%, is $16.7 billion or $20.2 billion, compared to NovaGold's current market capitalization on the NYSE of $4.45 billion. This discrepancy, favoring NovaGold's attractive valuation, could potentially trigger strong demand from market participants and thus drive the share price up on the NYSE.

The Risks

The first risk is a delay or bureaucratic hurdle in obtaining the remaining permits. Should this occur, the market could reassess the valuation and impair the inflow of additional capital essential for developing Donlin Gold into a gold mine. This risk exists although it is not elevated.

The second risk arises from a reversal of the gold price trend toward lower prices. This would negatively impact Donlin Gold's economic growth prospects and be reflected in the market valuation of NovaGold Resources' NYSE shares. This risk is currently low.

The third risk concerns NovaGold's available liquidity and the associated flexibility to meet its financing needs. NovaGold had approximately US$115.1 million in cash and cash equivalents as of November 30, 2025, the end of the fiscal year. This amount is in addition to the gross proceeds of approximately $310 million from the Upsized Bought Deal, which was finalized on February 6, 2026. This brings the total gross amount to $425.1 million, assuming no further expenditures have been incurred since the end of the fiscal year. NovaGold has approved a budget of $131.4 million for 2026, of which $78.8 million is allocated to Donlin Gold's activities, including advancing the feasibility study and obtaining permits. No financial difficulties are anticipated during this transition period until construction of the gold mine commences.

The average trading volume (3 months) of 3.42 million shares traded per day at the time of this article (Seeking Alpha's "Trading Data") is neither high nor excessively low. There is liquidity in the NYSE market.

Conclusion

NG NovaGold Resources is making progress on all fronts. Capital is flowing in from the stock market, and rulings by the Alaska Supreme Court uphold Donlin Gold's right to exploit water resources and other rights in the Kuskokwim and Mt. McKinley gold mining districts. Agreements are also in place to construct a gas pipeline to supply future production. A bankable feasibility study will be completed in 2027. The board of directors has experts with proven planning experience to further accelerate momentum. We are in the final stages of the permitting process. The price of gold is rising, and with this progress toward commencing construction and commercial production at Donlin Gold in 2031, NovaGold Resources shares on the NYSE are very promising.

data.title: MPLX: 12% Distribution Growth For Years To Come
data.description:
Co-authored by Kody's Dividends

Steady, growing passive income is the pathway we're taking to reach and preserve our financial freedom. We define financial freedom as the point at which passive income exceeds expenses (preferably with a modest buffer to endure an isolated dividend cut in a portfolio that occurs periodically).

Achieving this involves consistently living well below our active income (we routinely save 60%+ of our after-tax income). That's the engine powering our journey to the financial promised land.

But that surplus capital has to go somewhere, and this brings us to an equally important point. Having reliable income machines is what will get us across the finish line and maintain our financial sovereignty. We describe these as high-quality businesses with durable cash flows and a commitment to returning them to unitholders with military-grade consistency.

A core holding in our portfolios is MPLX LP (MPLX). When we last covered it with a "Buy" rating in November, we thought the partnership had plenty of organic growth projects to continue producing business growth. The company's investment-grade balance sheet was another positive. Another plus was the safety of its distribution. Clinching the buy case, units were modestly undervalued.

Fast forward a few months, and we're reiterating our "Buy" rating. The company cited promising U.S. natural demand growth forecasts as justification for a predominant focus on natural gas and NGL capex with its $2.4 billion organic growth capex plan for 2026. MPLX's leverage ratio remains viable. Exceptional distribution growth appears poised to persist. Finally, units are still reasonably valued.

The Long-Term Growth Outlook Remains Solid

Recently, MPLX released its financial results for the fourth quarter ended Dec. 31, 2025. The company's total operating revenue grew by 6.2% over the year-ago period to $3.25 billion in the quarter. This came in $70 million ahead of Seeking Alpha's analyst consensus during the quarter.

What was behind MPLX's topline growth for the fourth quarter? As one could imagine, there were several pieces to the puzzle.

In his opening remarks during the Q4 2025 Earnings Call, CFO Carl Hagedorn pointed to a revised FERC tariff issued in November (a $37 million benefit) and higher rates. Secondly, crude oil pipeline throughput edged 0.9% higher year-over-year to over 5.9 million barrels per day. Gathering throughput also increased by 1.7% over the year-ago period to more than 6.8 billion cubic feet per day. Third, the Northwind Midstream acquisition and a larger stake in the BANGL Pipeline offered new revenue streams in the Permian Basin.

These tailwinds countered two key headwinds: Lower NGL prices and asset divestitures of non-core gathering and processing assets in late 2025.

This led adjusted EBITDA to increase by 2.4% year-over-year to $1.80 billion in the fourth quarter. Higher interest expense from the debt assumed, mostly from the Northwind Midstream acquisition, was a near-term hit to distributable cash flow, with it decreasing by 4.1% over the year-ago period to $1.42 billion.

Still, we don't mind the near-term and temporary trade-off to DCF that happened during the fourth quarter. That's because MPLX is integrating Northwind's Delaware Basin assets into its network. In doing so, MPLX can now offer a one-stop shop for "sour gas" (natural gas with high levels of hydrogen sulfide) treatment to producers.

The ongoing construction of a $400 million facility (the Titan Complex) to treat 440 million cubic feet per day of sour gas (up from 150 million cubic feet per day) will be placed into service in Q4 2026. Upon its completion, this will provide a solution to producers who previously had to throttle back their drilling.

Along with a downstream feed into the BANGL Pipeline that MPLX now completely owns, the partnership will generate a fee on every barrel of NGL captured by Northwind via gathering, transportation (BANGL Pipeline), and frac/export (MPLX's Gulf Coast terminals). This is why we're confident this deal will pay off.

The more macro picture is just as promising for MPLX. President and CEO Maryann Mannen noted that U.S. natural gas demand will grow by a projected 15%+ through 2030. This will be powered by the expansion of LNG export capacity and rising power needs from data centers.

That's why MPLX is planning to invest $2.4 billion in organic growth capital in 2026, with about 90% of this going to its Natural Gas and NGL Services segment.

Another key natural gas and NGL infrastructure project includes the BANGL Pipeline expansion from 200,000 barrels per day to 300,000 barrels per day (supported by extra feed from Titan and the Secretariat II plant). This is scheduled to be completed in Q4 2026.

Harmon Creek III is expected to come online a quarter sooner, in Q3 2026. This 300 million cubic feet of added processing capacity will unleash a system that was basically running at its limit (97% Marcellus processing utilization).

These catalysts have MPLX set for a big 2026. The FAST Graphs analyst consensus is for adjusted EBITDA per unit to rocket 22% higher to $7.32. In 2027, another 7.5% uptick in adjusted EBITDA per unit to $7.87 is anticipated. For the three analyst forecasts thus far, it's forecasted that adjusted EBITDA per unit will increase by 4.7% to $8.24 (as more analyst estimates become available, this will probably rise further).

Financially, MPLX is also relatively stable. The total debt to LTM adjusted EBITDA ratio of 3.7x to close out 2025 was a bit elevated versus recent years. Still, as MPLX's key projects come online and the value of the Northwind acquisition is realized, the partnership expects leverage to go back down.

MPLX is also comfortable with a leverage ratio within 4x, so that leaves it a cushion in the meantime. Debt maturities are also reasonably well-staggered through 2030, with no more than $2 billion coming due in any given year. This is why S&P issues a BBB S&P credit rating with a stable outlook (unless otherwise sourced or hyperlinked, all info in this subhead was according to MPLX's Q4 2025 Earnings Press Release and MPLX's Q4 2025 Earnings Presentation).

Units Could Be Priced At Fair Value

Since we last covered MPLX in November, units have delivered a total return of just above 3%. This was slightly above the roughly 3% total return logged by the S&P 500 Index (SP500) over that time.

That price appreciation has MPLX situated right around our updated fair value estimate of 7.5. Units are trading at a forward 12-month P/EBITDA ratio of approximately 7.5.

Why are we basically maintaining our fair value P/EBITDA multiple of 7.5? Our rationale is two-pronged.

For one, MPLX's fundamentals appear to be stable. To this point, the partnership's annual adjusted EBITDA growth consensus of 10.8% is as strong as it has been at any point in recent years. For more context, its historical CAGR as a publicly traded company is 7.4%.

What's more, such a multiple is backed up by both the long-term historical average and the average in recent years. In its corporate history, the 13-year average P/EBITDA ratio was 8.4.

This includes the time before the 2015-2016 energy bear market, in which midstream companies relied heavily on unit issuances to drive growth. Now, with an entirely self-sustaining business model (retained DCF alone mostly covered growth capex), there's a case to be made that MPLX could continue to support such a valuation multiple.

However, we prefer a one standard deviation discount to the 13-year P/EBITDA multiple to leave an adequate margin of safety. That also jives with the average in recent years (the three-year average P/EBITDA ratio was also 7.5).

As of now, the calendar year 2026 is 10% complete. This implies that another 90% of 2026 and 10% of 2027 are yet to come over the next 12 months. That yields a forward 12-month adjusted EBITDA per unit input of $7.37.

Using our fair value P/EBITDA ratio of 7.5, we get a fair value of $55 a unit. This is also in line with the current unit price (as of February 4th, 2026), which signals the partnership is conservatively trading around fair value.

If units return to fair value and match growth forecasts, MPLX could generate an 8% total return in what's left of 2026. By the end of 2030, MPLX will have a path to 12%+ annual total returns.

A Safe and Fast-Growing High-Yield

MPLX's 7.8% forward distribution yield remains substantially higher than the energy sector's 3.5% median forward yield. Put into perspective, the partnership's yield is higher than all of its C-Corp midstream counterparts, including Enbridge's (ENB) 5.7% yield and ONEOK's (OKE) 5.5% yield. Among its MLP peer group, only Western Midstream's (WES) 8.9% yield and Plains GP Holdings' (PAGP) 8% yield are higher. That earns MPLX respective A- grades from Seeking Alpha's Quant System for forward distribution yield and overall distribution yield (the former of which is down from an A grade and the latter of which is up from a B+ grade in November).

For such a high yield, MPLX's starting income is very durable, too. In 2025, the partnership generated $5.79 billion in DCF. Against $4.14 billion in total distributions paid, this equates to a distribution coverage ratio of 1.4x (a DCF payout ratio in the low-70% range). According to the Zen Research Terminal, this is more secure than the 1.2x (~83% payout ratio) that rating agencies have set forth as the industry-safe standard.

For even more color, this left MPLX with $1.65 billion in retained DCF in 2025. By itself, this funded the vast majority of the company's $2.04 billion in organic growth capital expenditures during the year. That further speaks to the sustainability of MPLX's distribution.

This is why the Zen Research Terminal pegs the risk of a distribution reduction at just 0.8% within the next 12 months. In an average recession, that rises to a still manageable 3.5%.

Management is also optimistic about the future of distribution growth. Here's what they had to say about distribution growth for the foreseeable future:

Thanks, Chris. Through disciplined capital deployment, execution and optimization of our integrated value chains, we have achieved a 3-year adjusted EBITDA CAGR of 6.7%. This strong performance enabled us to increase our quarterly distribution by 12.5% for a consecutive year in 2025. We expect this level of distribution growth for 2 more years. MPLX enters 2026 in a position of strength.

- MPLX President and CEO Maryann Mannen

MPLX won't be sacrificing distribution sustainability, either. CFO Carl Hagedorn assured analysts on the Q4 2025 Earnings Call that he doesn't see annual distribution coverage going below the comfort level of at least 1.3x.

That's why we largely agree with the Quant System's forecast of 11.7% annual forward distribution per unit growth from MPLX. This would be well above the sector median of just 5%. That's sufficient for the Quant System to issue a B+ grade for the annual forward distribution per unit growth metric (unchanged from November). This would give MPLX a Chowder Number near the 20s for the next few years.

This would allow MPLX to emphatically build on what's already a 12-year distribution growth streak. That's better than the sector median of merely 2.1 years, which warrants an A+ grade from the Quant System for the consecutive years of distribution growth metric (the same as it was in November).

Risks to Consider

MPLX is a fundamentally strong partnership. Still, it's not without its risks.

As we outlined in November, the most pertinent risk to MPLX is its customer concentration. Marathon Petroleum (MPC) routinely makes up nearly half of MPLX's total revenue.

If the refining industry faces a downturn via falling crack spreads or a drop in refined product demand, MPC's ability to grow volume commitments could come under pressure. In turn, that would likely weigh on MPLX's ability to hit growth targets.

A similar risk would be the counterparty risk associated with this arrangement. If MPC encounters financial difficulties, this could significantly strain MPLX's financial results, prompting a distribution cut in a worst-case scenario.

Execution risk is another consideration. While 2025 was characterized by the $2.4 billion acquisition of Northwind Midstream, 2026 will be a building year. MPLX's growth hinges on the successful execution of its $2.4 billion organic growth plan for 2026. If any key projects (e.g., Harmon Creek III or the BANGL Pipeline expansion) hit snags, this could impede MPLX's growth profile.

One last risk to the partnership is that it's a high-value target for hackers. If any cyber breaches are successful, its operations could be disrupted. Aside from the immediate financial implications of such an event, the fallout could also include customer lawsuits and regulatory fines.

Conclusion

MPLX has earned a reputation as a top-notch MLP holding. The big picture points to a promising outlook for U.S. natural gas demand. This vindicates the partnership's heavy capex slant toward natural gas and NGL services. MPLX is financially healthy as well. The nearly 8% distribution yield is adequately covered by DCF. Mix in nearly 12% distribution growth for the next 2 years, if not longer, and MPLX becomes a dividend investor's dream opportunity.

Building a portfolio that meets your financial needs doesn't have to be difficult, but it does require diligence. Unfortunately, many investors fall to the wayside, attracted by high, unsustainable yields, looking for current income, and forgetting they'll need more cash down the road. This is something that we actively combat within our Dividend Kings' Model Portfolio. Yes, we want money today, but we're not going to neglect the money we will need tomorrow.

Cut through the chaos and find clarity by being a dividend king.

data.title: Allspring International Equity Fund Q4 2025 Performers And Detractors
data.description:
The following segment was excerpted from the Allspring International Equity Fund Q4 2025 Commentary.

Fund performance and attribution

Contributors

  • Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd. (SSNLF)
  • Barclays plc (BCS)
  • LVMH Moet Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE (LVMHF)
  • Deutsche Post AG (DHLGY)
  • Hitachi Ltd. (HTHIY)

Detractors

  • Tokio Marine Holdings, Inc. (TKOMY)
  • BAE Systems plc (BAESF)
  • Rheinmetall AG (RNMBF)
  • adidas AG (ADDYY)
  • SAP SE (SAP)

The holdings identified do not represent all of the securities purchased or sold during the period shown and should not be construed as a recommendation to purchase or sell a particular security. Information on calculation methodology and a list showing the overall contribution of each holding in the account for the period shown are available upon request.

The Allspring International Equity Fund outperformed its benchmark MSCI EAFE Index (NET) return (+4.8%) in the fourth quarter of 2025 wrapping up a strong year for EAFE, which delivered the highest annual performance relative to the S&P 500 Index in the past 30 years. As U.S. stocks broadly underperformed developed markets ex U.S. (MSCI EAFE Index [Net]) this period, the fund outperformed the MSCI World Index (NET) (+3.1%) and U.S. large-cap equities as measured by the S&P 500 Index (+2.7%).

The U.S. dollar was stable in the fourth quarter versus a basket of major world currencies after experiencing a significant decline during the first half of 2025 as trade policies spiked global economic uncertainty. On a year-to-date basis, the MSCI EAFE Index (NET) has outperformed the S&P 500 Index and continues to contribute important diversification characteristics to asset allocation.

The team's bottom-up, company-focused investment process typically results in stock selection being the primary driver of relative performance for the fund. Sector weighting differences are typically the result of balancing income goals with those of long-term capital appreciation.

From a sector perspective, stock selection was strongly positive from the IT sector, particularly Korean-domiciled Samsung Electronics Ltd. which enjoyed a strong recovery in 2025, and the consumer discretionary sector, led by recovery in LVMH Louis Vuitton. Stock selection was a negative contributor in the industrials sector, particularly defense companies Rheinmetall AG and BAE Systems plc, and in the consumer staples sector given weakness in beverage companies Asahi Group Holdings (ASBRF) and Diageo plc (DEO).

The lowest absolute returns in the fund this period were observed from Japanese communications/gaming company Nintendo Co. Ltd. (NTDOY); United Kingdom (U.K.)-domiciled defense company BAE Systems, plc; and Japanese insurance company Tokio Marine while absolute returns were highest from French luxury goods company LVMH Louis Vuitton, Japanese semiconductor equipment manufacturer Tokyo Electron, and U.K.-based bank Barclays plc.

On a geographic basis, the largest detractors (total effect) were the fund's holdings domiciled in Germany, including Rheinmetall and adidas. The largest contributor by country of domicile was from Korean-domiciled Samsung Electronics.

adidas AG (adidas), headquartered in Germany, designs and markets athletic footwear, apparel, and accessories globally, generating revenue through wholesale and direct-to-consumer channels. adidas faced a challenging quarter as elevated inventory levels and strategic early purchases weighed on operational agility despite strong brand momentum. Its scale, innovation pipeline, and global reach remain key differentiators that enable the company to capture demand across major regions and sporting events. adidas was a detractor from performance this quarter as inventory build and cost pressures tempered near-term flexibility. A headwind emerged from accelerated intake tied to future event merchandising, even as guidance was raised on sustained brand strength. In the months ahead, global sportswear demand offers opportunity, despite lingering concerns around inventory normalization.

BAE Systems plc (BAE) , the defense company headquartered in the U.K., underperformed the benchmark as the stock had strongly outperformed the market year to date coming into October, and the company gave its normal quarterly update without raising guidance, against high investor expectations. We also believe market speculation about a potential settlement in the Russia/Ukraine war caused investors to take gains in defense stocks. Nonetheless, we remain positive on BAE's prospects with international defense budgets continuing to move higher and the company reporting strong order growth during the third quarter and good prospects for the upcoming fourth quarter. The company is seeing particularly strong demand at present for missiles, air electronics, naval ships, and army vehicles.

Barclays plc (BCS) , a diversified financial services company headquartered in the U.K., was a contributor during the period. Barclays delivered strong third quarter results, marked by solid execution and operational progress in its U.S. credit card and U.K. retail businesses. Management increased its 2025 return on equity and net interest income guidance. An accelerated share buyback and recent dividend increase signal confidence in capital return priorities. Momentum around lending and deposit growth creates room for the company to meet or exceed its long-term guidance.

LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE (LVMH) , domiciled in France, operates as a global leader in luxury goods spanning fashion, leather goods, wines and spirits, perfumes, cosmetics, watches, jewelry, and selective retailing. In the fourth quarter, LVMH delivered improved momentum, with Sephora continuing to outperform peers and selective retailing driving growth, while fashion and leather goods stabilized after prior softness. Its extensive brand portfolio and strong retail execution underpin resilience, supported by creative renewal and network upgrades that enhance customer engagement. Relative to the market, LVMH outperformed this quarter, aided by broad-based recovery across segments and disciplined cost management. A key catalyst was the successful launch of new collections and retail initiatives, which lifted traffic and volumes even as currency headwinds persisted. If we see a recovery in the Chinese consumer this year, it would be a significant catalyst to get sales back to growing closer to the mid-to-high-single-digit range in its largest division (fashion and leather goods), leading to margin improvement and earnings accretion, which we believe is another leg to the story in the coming year.

Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd. (Samsung Electronics) , based in South Korea, operates as a global leader in semiconductors, consumer electronics, and mobile devices, generating revenue through memory solutions, foundry services, and advanced technology products. Samsung Electronics strengthened its position this quarter by deepening collaboration with NVIDIA on next-generation memory technologies and AI-driven manufacturing initiatives. Its ability to combine cutting-edge DRAM and logic designs with large-scale production capacity underscores a competitive advantage in meeting surging AI-related demand. Samsung Electronics outperformed the benchmark this quarter, supported by robust semiconductor momentum, strong pricing for memory, and strategic partnerships. In the months ahead, expanding AI infrastructure creates room for upside, while supply constraints and execution risks remain on the radar.

Tokio Marine Holdings, Inc. (Tokio Marine) , a global insurance group headquartered in Japan, was a detractor during the period. Tokio Marine faced a challenging third quarter as softer premium growth in its international businesses and increased advertising costs weighed on profitability, alongside a downward revision in its Asia Life business outlook. A slight downgrade to full-year guidance and cautious stance on catastrophe risk added to headwinds. The Japan property and casualty business should continue to see increasing premium rates with continued good underwriting performance from the international businesses.

Benchmark descriptions:

The Lipper averages are compiled by Lipper, Inc., an independent mutual fund research and rating service. Each Lipper average represents a universe of funds that are similar in investment objective. You cannot invest directly in a Lipper average.

The Morningstar Category average is the average return for the peer group based on the returns of each individual fund within the group. The total return of the Morningstar Category average does not include the effect of sales charges. You cannot invest directly in a Morningstar Category average.

The Morgan Stanley Capital International (MSCI) Europe, Australasia, Far East (EAFE) Index (NET) is a free-float-adjusted market-capitalization-weighted index that is designed to measure the equity market performance of developed markets, excluding the U.S. and Canada. You cannot invest directly in an index.

The Morgan Stanley Capital International (MSCI) All Country World Index (ACWI) ex USA (NET) is a free-float-adjusted market-capitalization-weighted index that is designed to measure the equity market performance of developed and emerging markets, excluding the U.S. You cannot invest directly in an index.

The Morgan Stanley Capital International (MSCI) World Index (NET) is a free-float-adjusted market-capitalization-weighted index that is designed to measure the equity market performance of developed markets. You cannot invest directly in an index.

The Morgan Stanley Capital International (MSCI) Europe Index (NET) captures large- and mid-cap representation across 15 developed markets countries in Europe. Countries include: Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Ireland, Italy, the Netherlands, Norway, Portugal, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, and the United Kingdom. With 424 constituents, the index covers approximately 85% of the free-float-adjusted market capitalization across the European developed markets equity universe. You cannot invest directly in an index.

Source: MSCI. MSCI makes no express or implied warranties or representations and shall have no liability whatsoever with respect to any MSCI data contained herein. The MSCI data may not be further redistributed or used as a basis for other indexes or any securities or financial products. This report is not approved, reviewed, or produced by MSCI.

The MSCI Japan Index is designed to measure the performance of the large- and mid-cap segments of the Japanese market. With 180 constituents, the index covers approximately 85% of the free float-adjusted market capitalization in Japan. You cannot invest directly in an index.

The S&P 500 Index consists of 500 stocks chosen for market size, liquidity, and industry group representation. It is a market-value-weighted index with each stock's weight in the index proportionate to its market value. You cannot invest directly in an index.

Diversification does not ensure or guarantee better performance and cannot eliminate the risk of investment losses.

Attribution analysis:

Performance attribution and sector and country returns are calculated using the Brinson-Fachler attribution model. As a result, performance attribution calculations may differ from the fund's actual investment results. Common sources of attribution dispersion from official returns include: the use of end of day holdings and prices, the exclusion of fees, and the use of myriad pricing sources to accommodate different benchmark providers.

Definition of terms:

Alpha measures the difference between a fund's actual returns and its expected performance given its level of risk (as measured by beta).

Beta measures fund volatility relative to general market movements. It is a standardized measure of systematic risk in comparison with a specified index. The benchmark beta is 1.00 by definition.

Downside capture measures a fund's replication of its benchmark during periods of negative returns. During periods of negative benchmark returns, a downside capture ratio less than 100% reflects product performance greater than the benchmark and a downside capture ratio greater than 100% reflects performance less than the benchmark.

Information ratio measures the consistency of excess return (return in excess of a benchmark). This value is determined by taking the annualized excess return over a benchmark (style benchmark by default) and dividing it by the standard deviation of excess return.

Price/earnings (P/E) is the price of a share of a stock divided by earnings per share, usually calculated using the latest year's earnings.

Price book ratio is calculated by dividing the current price of a company's stock times its shares outstanding (market capitalization) by its last quarter's book value. Book value is assets less liabilities which is equivalent to book value of equity. This ratio represents the market value for every dollar of tangible assets.

R-squared is a measurement of how similar a fund's historical performance has been to that of the benchmark. The measure ranges from 0.0, which means that the fund's performance bears no relationship to the performance of the index, to 1.0, which means that the fund's performance was perfectly synchronized with the performance of the benchmark.

Sharpe ratio measures the potential reward offered by a mutual fund relative to its risk level. The ratio uses a fund's standard deviation and its excess return to determine reward per unit of risk. The higher the Sharpe ratio, the better the fund's historical risk-adjusted performance.

Standard deviation represents the degree to which an investment's performance has varied from its average performance over a particular time period.

Tracking error measures the extent to which a manager's performance mimics that of a benchmark. The value is the standard deviation of the difference between a fund's performance and a benchmark's performance.

Upside capture measures a fund's replication of its benchmark during periods of positive returns. During periods of positive benchmark returns, an upside capture ratio greater than 100% reflects product performance greater than the benchmark and an upside capture ratio less than 100% reflects performance less than the benchmark.

The Morningstar Rating for funds, or star rating, is calculated for managed products (including mutual funds, variable annuity and variable life subaccounts, exchange-traded funds, closed-end funds, and separate accounts) with at least a three-year history. Exchange-traded funds and open-ended mutual funds are considered a single population for comparative purposes. It is calculated based on a Morningstar risk-adjusted return measure that accounts for variation in a managed product's monthly excess performance, placing more emphasis on downward variations and rewarding consistent performance. The Morningstar Rating does not include any adjustment for sales loads. The top 10% of products in each product category receive 5 stars, the next 22.5% receive 4 stars, the next 35% receive 3 stars, the next 22.5% receive 2 stars, and the bottom 10% receive 1 star. The Overall Morningstar Rating for a managed product is derived from a weighted average of the performance figures associated with its 3-, 5-, and 10-year (if applicable) Morningstar Rating metrics. The weights are: 100% 3-year rating for 36–59 months of total returns, 60% 5-year rating/40% 3-year rating for 60–119 months of total returns, and 50% 10-year rating/30% 5-year rating/20% 3-year rating for 120 or more months of total returns. While the 10-year overall star rating formula seems to give the most weight to the 10-year period, the most recent 3-year period actually has the greatest impact because it is included in all three rating periods.

Across U.S.-domiciled foreign large blend funds, the International Equity Fund received 2 stars among 644 funds, 3 stars among 610 funds, and 1 star among 479 funds for the 3-, 5-, and 10-year periods, respectively. The Morningstar Rating is for the Institutional Class only; other classes may have different performance characteristics.

The Morningstar absolute ranking is based on the fund's total return rank relative to all funds that have the same category for the same time period. Morningstar rankings do not include the effect of sales charges. Past performance is no guarantee of future results.
